items.final_wording: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

items.rationale: The exact DHA burn mechanism and classification section supports onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism only when discussed or assessed; it does not classify the burn automatically.

items.final_wording: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — airway, vital signs, depth, site, size, and total body surface area

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

items.rationale: The exact assessment section supports recording measured vital data, focused examination, injury severity and appearance only when clinician-assessed; no depth or area is inferred.

items.final_wording: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

items.rationale: The exact red-flag and referral section supports documenting time-critical features and escalation only when assessed or decided.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led burn care, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, monitoring, and follow-up

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.

items.rationale: The exact management and follow-up section supports recording only a clinician-entered plan and arranged handover or follow-up; treatment content is not generated.
